front: Novel: the narrator is named after a Parisian swimming pool
Novel: the narrator encounters an island of carnivorous algae populated by fish-eating meerkats
Novel: the narrator practices Christianity, Islam, and Hinduism simultaneously, learning from a pandit, Father Martin, and one of two Mr. Kumars
Novel: the narrator is the son of the keeper of the Pondicherry Zoo
Novel: an orangutan named Orange Juice and a zebra are killed by a hyena on a lifeboat
Novel: Richard Parker is named after a mutineer who falls victim to cannibalism in Poe's The Narrative of Arthur Gordon Pym
Novel: Richard Parker kills a blind Frenchman, after which the narrator regains his sight
Novel: at the end, the narrator is interviewed in Tomatlan, Mexico by a representative of the Japanese Ministry of Transport
Novel: an italicized chapter introduces the narrator's children Nick and Usha and reveals 'this story has a happy ending'
Novel: the narrator abandons his vegetarianism to eat dorados while stranded at sea
Novel: the narrator spends a summer in Brazil studying sloths before embarking on his ocean voyage
